description: You are the configuration manager for a small service-deployment project. Inside the working directory /home/user/configs you will find two pre-existing files: 1. /home/user/configs/app_config.json – a full configuration document. 2. /home/user/configs/app_schema.json – a JSON-Schema (draft-07) that the configuration must satisfy. Your job consists of three very small shell operations (2–3 commands in total are enough): 1. Validate app_config.json against app_schema.json. 2. Using jq, create a distilled configuration extract called /home/user/configs/app_summary.json that contains only the keys shown below. 3. Append one fixed, single-line entry to /home/user/configs/change.log documenting the validation. IMPORTANT: • If validation fails, do nothing else. If validation succeeds, continue with steps 2 & 3. • The summary file must have precisely the following JSON structure and whitespace (so the grader can diff it byte-for-byte): { "version": "1.2.3", "services": [ { "name": "web", "enabled": true }, { "name": "db", "enabled": false } ] } • The log line you add must be the literal text below (followed by a single UNIX newline): 2024-01-01T00:00:00Z validation_success • Place the summary file in the same directory as the originals and leave all other files untouched. • Only 2 or 3 straightforward shell commands are expected; do not script anything longer. When you finish, the system will be checked for: 1. Existence and contents of /home/user/configs/app_summary.json. 2. A new last line in /home/user/configs/change.log matching the fixed timestamp and wording exactly. 3. That app_config.json conforms to app_schema.json (so your validation step must have succeeded).
